expectations.text: Names the visual hierarchy problem explicitly
expectations.evidence: 'The problem is flat visual hierarchy — every element on your page is competing for attention at the same visual weight'

expectations.text: Mentions at least two of the three hierarchy tools: font size, font weight, color/contrast
expectations.evidence: 'Use all three hierarchy tools together — font size, font weight, and color'

expectations.text: Discusses different levels of content importance (primary, secondary, tertiary)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly grades stat values as primary (large, bold, dark) and labels as secondary (small, 12px, muted grey); also distinguishes primary/secondary table columns

expectations.text: Gives specific actionable recommendations with values (e.g., font weights 600-700, grey for secondary text)
expectations.evidence: Stat value: '32–40px, heavy weight (700)'; stat label: '12px, normal weight, muted grey (#6B7280)'; title: '28–32px, bold (700)'

claims.claim: Hierarchy can be fixed without changing layout
claims.evidence: Response explicitly states 'These changes don't require a redesign. They're purely typographic'

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are solid and discriminating. The fourth assertion effectively distinguishes skill-level specificity from generic advice.
